data.content_ref: inline:The target's mechanism derivation is internally inconsistent and mathematically unjustified. It claims that the condition 'R > 1-e and P > e' guarantees net benefit because 'the critic's positive likelihood ratio exceeds the class prior ratio'. However, the positive likelihood ratio (LR+) is defined as TPR/FPR = R/(1-P). The target's condition P > e does NOT imply R/(1-P) > P_inc/P_cor. In fact, P > e implies 1-P < 1-e, so the denominator of LR+ is strictly bounded by 1-e. For the LR+ to exceed the prior ratio of ~2.45 (0.71/0.29), one needs R/(1-P) > 2.45. The target's condition R > 1-e only gives R/(1-P) > (1-e)/(1-P). Since P > e, 1-P < 1-e, so (1-e)/(1-P) > 1, but this does NOT guarantee it exceeds 2.45. The simplification 'R/P > 2.45/(R+...)' is nonsensical and the conclusion that 'R > 1-e and P > e' guarantees net benefit is a non-sequitur. The actual required condition is R/(1-P) > N_cor/N_inc ≈ 2.45, which is a strictly stronger constraint on the combination of R and P than either parameter individually exceeding e or 1-e. The predicted regime is therefore fundamentally flawed.
